scenario_title: Duplicate add_rr for the same RRset member in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test isolates intra-message duplication by using an empty target name and a single UPDATE with two identical add_rr operations for the same owner, type, and rdata. A server that normalizes duplicate additions may return NOERROR and leave one RR present, while a server that validates duplicate insertion as a conflict may reject the UPDATE and leave the name absent. The follow-up QUERY on y.campus.edu. A makes the result binary and observable.

group_1.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE and stores a single A RR for y.campus.edu. after coalescing or treating the second identical add as a no-op.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE or otherwise handles the duplicate add as a conflict, leaving no A RR at y.campus.edu.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 defines add_rr encoding and general UPDATE processing, but it does not specify whether two identical additions to the same RRset within one UPDATE are coalesced, applied sequentially, or treated as a duplicate conflict. That leaves implementations free to differ in both the UPDATE RCODE and the resulting RRset state.
